channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Why Risk Aircraft When Missiles Do the Same Job? Lt Gen P R Shankar’s Powerful Lessons From War in Ops Sindoor
item.description: In the high-stakes arena of modern conflict, retired Lieutenant General P R Shankar has sparked intense debate with a straightforward yet provocative question: why risk aircraft and pilots when precision missiles can deliver identical results with far less exposure? His analysis, drawn directly from the hard lessons of recent operations including Ops Sindoor, challenges long-held assumptions about air power and urges a fundamental rethink of how India approaches precision strikes against static targets deep inside adversary territory.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: OEF–SCARPA Deal to Localize Snow Boots for Indian Army, Targeting 50% Cost Reduction
item.description: In a move aligned with India’s broader push for defence indigenization, the Ordnance Equipment Factory (OEF) has signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Italian outdoor footwear specialist SCARPA to manufacture high-altitude snow boots for the Indian Army at its Kanpur facility. The collaboration targets a significant reduction in procurement costs while enhancing supply chain resilience for troops deployed in extreme environments.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IndiGo Picks Stake in Air Taxi Startup Sarla Aviation
item.description: IndiGo, through its investment arm IndiGo Ventures, has acquired a ?10 crore equity stake in the Bengaluru-based air taxi startup Sarla Aviation. This strategic move marks IndiGo’s direct entry into India’s emerging urban air mobility (UAM) sector after its previous partnership with US-based Archer Aviation fell through due to delivery timeline delays.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Designate Hamas as terror outfit: Israel FM Sa’ar to India
item.description: Israeli Foreign Minister Gideon Sa’ar has urged the Indian government to formally designate Hamas as a terrorist organisation, specifically highlighting alleged operational links between Hamas and the Pakistan-based terror group Lashkar-e-Taiba (LeT).
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Jeh Aerospace signs Long-Term Agreement with Liebherr-Aerospace for precision landing gear components
item.description: Jeh Aerospace has signed a Long-Term Agreement (LTA) with Liebherr-Aerospace to manufacture and supply high-precision components for landing gear systems on high-rate commercial single-aisle aircraft programs. Under the agreement, Jeh Aerospace will manufacture and supply these components from its dedicated facility in Hyderabad, India, thus becoming part of Liebherr-Aerospace’s global industrial network.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Had a very good conversation’: Trump on phone call with ‘friend’ PM Modi
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Friday said he had a “very good conversation” with his “friend” Prime Minister Narendra Modi. He was referring to the talks the two leaders had on Tuesday. “I had a very good talk with him, and he’s a friend of mine from India, and he’s doing great. We had a very good conversation,” Trump told reporters here as he headed to Las Vegas to address a roundtable on tax cuts.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Might go to Pakistan to sign deal if agreement reached with Iran’: Trump
item.description: US President Donald Trump on Thursday once again claimed that progress is being made in talks with Iran and suggested he could be involved in the signing of a peace agreement, if one is reached.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Army-Led Forces Destroy 10 Illegal Bunkers In Manipur
item.description: The Indian Army, in a joint operation with other security agencies, destroyed ten illegally set up bunkers and firing positions in Manipur’s Jalenbung Hills and Thoyee Heights, officials said on Thursday. Defence spokesman Lt Col Mahendra Rawat stated that, as part of ongoing operations along the Imphal-Dimapur National Highway (NH-2), joint security forces led by the Army’s Red Shield Division launched coordinated area domination patrols in the Jalenbung Hills and Thoyee Heights. The operation aimed to maintain security and prevent the re-establishment of hostile infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, Russia Review Bilateral Engagement, Explore Deeper Cooperation In Steel Sector
item.description: India and Russia on Thursday reviewed their ongoing engagement in the steel sector and discussed ways to deepen bilateral cooperation during a high-level round table held at the Ministry of Steel in the national capital.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Three held for spreading ‘misinformation’ on social media in J-K’s Baramulla
item.description: Three persons were arrested in Jammu and Kashmir’s Baramulla district for allegedly spreading rumours through various social media with the intent of disturbing peace, police said on Thursday. Police in Sopore area of the north Kashmir district arrested three persons for spreading misinformation and sharing old videos by portraying them as recent incidents, with the intent to disturb public peace and order, a police spokesman said.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: IAF’s Firm Backing Positions HAL’s HLFT-42 as Cornerstone of Post-2035 Training and Combat Doctrine
item.description: With Hindustan Aeronautics Limited receiving unequivocal backing from the Indian Air Force, the HLFT-42 programme is emerging as one of the most consequential indigenous aviation projects aimed at reshaping India’s pilot training and light combat ecosystem beyond 2035. According to information shared with idrw.org, despite the programme not yet clearing its Critical Design Review (CDR) milestone, the IAF has already indicated intent for firm orders of at least 100 aircraft, with the final number potentially going higher.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India’s New Submarine Coating Technology Improves Stealth and Survivability in Naval Warfare
item.description: India’s push toward self-reliance in critical naval technologies has seen steady progress, and one of the most strategically significant yet less-discussed developments is the indigenous production of rubber tiles for submarines. These specialized synthetic tiles, applied to the outer hull of submarines, play a decisive role in enhancing acoustic stealth—one of the most crucial factors in underwater warfare.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Advanced Assembly Jig at Nashik Set to Streamline Tejas Mk1A Fighter Production
item.description: India’s push to accelerate the production of its indigenous Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas Mk1A received a major boost last year after Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) operationalised a third production line equipped with advanced indigenous assembly infrastructure. The new facility, established at HAL’s Nashik division, significantly expands the production ecosystem for the Tejas programme and increases the overall manufacturing capacity to 24 aircraft per year, a key milestone for meeting the Indian Air Force’s growing requirements.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Media Leak Triggers Political Storm in Indonesia Over US Military Plan
item.description: A major geopolitical controversy has erupted after a classified U.S. proposal granting American military aircraft unrestricted access to Indonesian airspace was leaked just a day before a key defence agreement between Washington and Jakarta was due to be signed on April 13. The fallout forced both sides to exclude the overflight access clause from the final agreement, significantly altering the scope of what was intended to be a major strategic breakthrough in the Indo-Pacific.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Researcher Flags Inlet Design Constraints in Tejas Mk1 After Dubai Incident
item.description: A new technical study byAyush Sreejith, an independent researcher from Kerala, has brought fresh analytical insight into the loss of an Indian Air Force Tejas Mk1 during the Dubai Airshow in November 2025, pointing toward potential aerodynamic and thermal limitations in the aircraft’s inlet design under extreme flight conditions.
item.dc:creator: admin
